commit.message:
test: add fuzz tests for httpclient and retry (#118)

httpclient:
- FuzzNew: fuzz client construction with arbitrary options
- FuzzDoRequest: fuzz HTTP requests with arbitrary methods/paths
- FuzzBackoff: fuzz backoff calculation with edge-case durations

retry:
- FuzzCalcDelay: fuzz delay calculation with extreme multipliers/jitter
- FuzzDo: fuzz retry execution with varying attempts and delays

All targets verify no panics and non-negative delay values.

Co-authored-by: Jordan <devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(ui): 「付箋」→「ルーティン」に用語変更

全画面・コメントの「付箋」を「ルーティン」に統一。

Co-authored-by: jog1t <39823706+jog1t@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: dont allow configuration of runner key

* feat: add KV range operations (listRange, deleteRange, scan) (#4372)

* fix: correct anyhow imports and document KV range operation limits

* fix(kv): enforce default list limit of 16384 in file-system and cloudflare drivers

* chore(kv): remove scan alias for listRange

* fix(kv): bump TS runner protocol version to 7 and restore published bare schemas

The TypeScript runner's PROTOCOL_VERSION was still 6 while the protocol
file had been regenerated with v7 wire format, causing a wire mismatch.
Also restores v4/v5/v6.bare to their published state and adds v7.bare as
the new schema version. Updates CLAUDE.md with a rule to keep both Rust
and TypeScript protocol versions in sync.

* refactor(workflow-engine): optimize loop history pruning (#4370)

* fix: simplify loop history management and defer checkpoint flushes

Consolidate historyEvery and historyKeep config options into commitInterval to simplify the API. Add incremental compaction tracking via lastForgottenUpTo to avoid re-scanning already-forgotten iterations. Defer checkpoint flushes to run in parallel with the next iteration's user code. Introduce PendingDeletions interface for collecting deletions to execute alongside checkpoint writes. Expand test coverage for compaction behavior, crash recovery, and queue timeout edge cases. Update all documentation to reflect the simplified API.

* refactor(workflow-engine): rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval and add historySize

Rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval to clarify that it controls how often old iterations are pruned. Add separate historySize option to control how many iterations are retained, defaulting to historyPruneInterval. Rename internal method collectLoopCompaction to collectLoopPruning and update all terminology from compact/checkpoint to prune throughout comments, tests, and docs.

* refactor(workflow-engine): use range deletions for loop history pruning

Replace per-iteration prefix deletes with a single deleteRange call
and track lastPrunedUpTo to avoid redundant re-scans. Add deleteRange
to EngineDriver interface with implementations in both test and
production drivers.

commit.message:
feat(winmd-api-search): add WinMD cache generator script and related files (#45606)

<!-- Enter a brief description/summary of your PR here. What does it
fix/what does it change/how was it tested (even manually, if necessary)?
-->
## Summary of the Pull Request

Adds a new Copilot agent skill (`winmd-api-search`) that lets AI agents
discover and explore Windows desktop APIs by searching a local cache of
WinMD metadata. The skill covers Windows Platform SDK, WinAppSDK/WinUI,
NuGet package WinMDs, and project-output WinMDs — providing full API
surface details (types, members, enumeration values, namespaces) without
needing external documentation lookups.

**Key components:**

- `.github/skills/winmd-api-search/SKILL.md` — Skill definition with
usage instructions, search/detail workflows, and scoring guidance
- `scripts/Invoke-WinMdQuery.ps1` — PowerShell query engine supporting
actions: `search`, `type`, `members`, `enums`, `namespaces`, `stats`,
`projects`
- `scripts/Update-WinMdCache.ps1` — Orchestrator that builds the C#
cache generator, discovers project files, and runs the generator
- `scripts/cache-generator/CacheGenerator.csproj` + `Program.cs` — .NET
console app using `System.Reflection.Metadata` to parse WinMD files from
NuGet packages, project references, Windows SDK, and packages.config
into per-package JSON caches
- `scripts/cache-generator/Directory.Build.props`,
`Directory.Build.targets`, `Directory.Packages.props` — Empty isolation
files to prevent repo-level Central Package Management and build targets
from interfering with this standalone tool

<!-- Please review the items on the PR checklist before submitting-->
## PR Checklist

- [ ] Closes: #xxx <!-- Replace with issue number if applicable -->
- [x] **Communication:** I've discussed this with core contributors
already. If the work hasn't been agreed, this work might be rejected
- [ ] **Tests:** Added/updated and all pass — N/A: This is an offline
agent skill (PowerShell + standalone .NET tool) with no integration into
the main product build or runtime. Validated manually by running the
cache generator across multiple project contexts (ColorPickerUI,
CmdPal.UI, runner, ImageResizer, etc.) and exercising all query actions.
- [ ] **Localization:** All end-user-facing strings can be localized —
N/A: No end-user-facing strings; this is an internal developer/agent
tool
- [ ] **Dev docs:** Added/updated — The SKILL.md itself serves as the
documentation
- [ ] **New binaries:** Added on the required places — N/A: The cache
generator is a standalone dev-time tool, not shipped in the installer
- [ ] **Documentation updated:** If checked, please file a pull request
on [our docs
repo](https://github.com/MicrosoftDocs/windows-uwp/tree/docs/hub/powertoys)
and link it here: N/A

<!-- Provide a more detailed description of the PR, other things fixed,
or any additional comments/features here -->
## Detailed Description of the Pull Request / Additional comments

### Cache Generator (`Program.cs`, ~1000 lines)

A self-contained .NET console app that:

1. **Discovers WinMD sources** from four channels:
   - `project.assets.json` (PackageReference — modern .csproj/.vcxproj)
   - `packages.config` (legacy NuGet format)
- `<ProjectReference>` bin/ output (class libraries producing `.winmd`)
   - Windows SDK `UnionMetadata/` (highest installed version)

2. **Parses WinMD files** using `System.Reflection.Metadata` /
`PEReader` to extract:
- Types (classes, structs, interfaces, enums, delegates) with full
namespace
- Members (methods with decoded signatures/parameters, properties with
accessors, events)
   - Enum values
   - Base types and type kinds

3. **Outputs per-package JSON** under `Generated Files/winmd-cache/`:
- `packages/<Id>/<Version>/meta.json` — package summary
(type/member/namespace counts)
   - `packages/<Id>/<Version>/namespaces.json` — ordered namespace list
- `packages/<Id>/<Version>/types/<Namespace>.json` — full type detail
per namespace
- `projects/<ProjectName>.json` — maps each project to its package set

4. **Deduplicates** at the package level — if a package+version is
already cached, it's skipped on subsequent runs.

### Build Isolation

Three empty MSBuild files (`Directory.Build.props`,
`Directory.Build.targets`, `Directory.Packages.props`) in the
cache-generator folder prevent the repo's Central Package Management and
shared build configuration from interfering with this standalone tool.

### Query Engine (`Invoke-WinMdQuery.ps1`)

Supports seven actions: `search` (fuzzy text search across
types/members), `type` (full detail for a specific type), `members`
(filtered members of a type), `enums` (enumeration values), `namespaces`
(list all namespaces), `stats` (cache statistics), and `projects` (list
cached projects with their packages).

<!-- Describe how you validated the behavior. Add automated tests
wherever possible, but list manual validation steps taken as well -->
## Validation Steps Performed

1. **Cache generation:** Ran `Update-WinMdCache.ps1` across 310+ project
files in the repo — 8 packages parsed, 316 reused from cache, all
completed without errors
2. **Query testing on multiple projects:**
   - `ColorPickerUI` — verified Windows SDK baseline (7,023 types)
- `Microsoft.CmdPal.UI.ViewModels` (after restore) — verified 13
packages, 49,799 types, 112,131 members including WinAppSDK,
AdaptiveCards, CsWinRT, Win32Metadata
   - `runner` (C++ vcxproj) — verified packages.config fallback path
   - `ImageResizerExt` — verified project reference WinMD discovery
3. **All seven query actions validated:** `stats`, `search`,
`namespaces`, `type`, `enums`, `members`, `projects` — all returned
correct results
4. **Spell-check compliance:** SKILL.md vocabulary reviewed against
repo's check-spelling dictionaries; replaced flagged words with standard
alternatives

---------

commit.message:
Fix CI: restore DisableImplicitFSharpCoreReference + pin FSharp.Core 9.0.303

Without an explicit FSharp.Core PackageReference, NuGet restore produces
an assets file missing net9.0/net10.0 targets on SDK 10.0.200. Restoring
DisableImplicitFSharpCoreReference=true and pinning to a specific stable
version (9.0.303, not floating 9.0.*) forces NuGet to explicitly resolve
FSharp.Core for all three TFMs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Android: fix treble clef not displaying on staff

Use drawIntoCanvas with native android.graphics.Paint instead of
Compose TextMeasurer for the treble clef character (U+1D11E).
Android's native text system properly falls back to Noto Sans Symbols
which covers the Musical Symbols Unicode block.

commit.message:
fix: gauge bar shows 100% when contribution room is Full or Over-limit

When totalAvailableRoom is 0 (e.g. FHSA lifetime limit fully consumed),
the old formula `totalAvailableRoom > 0 ? ... : 0` always produced 0%
even though room_status was "full". This caused the visible bug where
the card said "Full" but the bar showed 0%.

Fix: compute roomStatus before the percent, then short-circuit to 100
whenever roomStatus is "full" or "over-limit". Applied to all 6 gauge
sites: TFSA/RRSP/FHSA dashboard cards in overview.js and each detail
page (tfsa.js, rrsp.js, fhsa.js).

Also remove the last remaining backward-compat patterns in overview.js
(missed by the previous cleanup): `?? taxableExcessAmount`,
`typeof data.is_over_contributed === "boolean"`, `?? Math.max(...)` for
cra_over_contribution_amount / cushion_used_amount, and
`typeof data.is_using_cushion === "boolean"`.

feat(testkit): add AssertErrorIs and AssertErrorAs helpers (#117)

Add two new assertion functions for structured error checking:

- AssertErrorIs: wraps errors.Is for sentinel error matching
  (e.g., io.EOF, context.Canceled, wrapped errors)
- AssertErrorAs: wraps errors.As for error type extraction
  (e.g., *apperror.Error, *url.Error)

Both functions include nil-error checks with clear failure messages.
AssertErrorAs validates the target is a non-nil pointer.

Full test coverage for pass, fail, nil, and invalid-target cases.

* Feature/aviationstack primary (#13)

* feat(worker): switch primary API to AviationStack with flight_date + 30d KV cache

- Remove AirLabs dependency; AviationStack is now the sole upstream API
- Add flight_date param to enable historical flight queries (key use case:
  pilots fill logbook after landing when flight is no longer 'live')
- Extend KV TTL from 7d to 30d to protect the 500 req/month free quota
- Worker acts as HTTPHTTPS proxy: App always uses HTTPS to reach Worker,
  Worker calls AviationStack via plain http:// (free-tier limitation),
  bypassing iOS ATS and Android Cleartext Traffic restrictions
- Add X-Cache: HIT/MISS response header for cache verification
- Validate date param format (YYYY-MM-DD) at Worker level
- Rename env secret AVIATIONSTACK_KEY  AVIATIONSTACK_API_KEY
- No App-side changes needed: ApiService.ts date format already YYYY-MM-DD

* fix(worker): replace broken ICAO slice with native flight_icao param + add limit=1

Bug: CCA1501  slice(0,2)+slice(3)  CC1501 (Macair), causing 100% miss
     for all Chinese 3-letter ICAO designators (CCA/CES/CSN etc.)

Fix: detect IATA(2-letter) vs ICAO(3-letter) via regex and dynamically
     choose flight_iata vs flight_icao AviationStack param  no string
     manipulation, AviationStack handles the lookup natively.

Also: add &limit=1 to minimise JSON payload size and response latency.

* fix(worker): use https:// for AviationStack per official docs (Basic Plan+)

Official API docs clearly state base URL is https://api.aviationstack.com/v1/
The previous http:// assumption was based on outdated free-tier reports.
Basic Plan and above supports HTTPS natively  full end-to-end TLS now.

* security: remove plaintext API key from wrangler.toml

Key must be stored as an encrypted Cloudflare Secret, not a [vars] entry.
Use: wrangler secret put AVIATIONSTACK_API_KEY

* chore: update compatibility_date to 2026-03-05; ignore .wrangler/ dir

* chore: ignore wrangler local cache dir

* feat(worker): enhance flight data gateway with multi-tier fallback and API key management

* feat(phase7): data migration & cloud sync

Phase 7.1 - Excel bulk import engine (ImportService.ts):
- expo-document-picker for cross-platform file selection
- Web/Native dual-path file read (ArrayBuffer vs Base64)
- Fingerprint-based idempotent dedup [导入] prefix in remarks
- QA pre-import warning when existing [导入] records detected
- Chunk-based batch write 1000 rows per transaction (perf guard)
- generateImportTemplate() standard 29-column xlsx template
- checkExistingImports() count query for pre-flight check

Phase 7.2 - Supabase cloud sync (SyncService.ts + supabaseClient.ts):
- WatermelonDB synchronize() bidirectional pull/push engine
- Supabase placeholder config with full RLS SQL in comments
- isSupabaseConfigured() guard - graceful local-only fallback
- Module-level sync status (avoids WatermelonDB getLocal type gap)

UI changes:
- SettingsScreen: +历史数据导入 section (download template + import)
- SettingsScreen: +云端同步 section (sync button + isConfigured state)
- DashboardScreen: sync status dot indicator (///) in header
  using useFocusEffect to refresh on screen focus

Tests & infra:
- __mocks__: added @supabase/supabase-js, @nozbe/watermelondb/sync,
  expo-document-picker, react-native stubs
- jest.config.js: mapped new mocks, excluded I/O-layer files from
  coverage thresholds (integration-test boundary)
- 3 new test files: supabaseClient.test.ts, SyncService.test.ts,
  ImportService.test.ts  (230 tests pass, all thresholds met)

* Feature/aviationstack primary (#13)

* feat(worker): switch primary API to AviationStack with flight_date + 30d KV cache

- Remove AirLabs dependency; AviationStack is now the sole upstream API
- Add flight_date param to enable historical flight queries (key use case:
  pilots fill logbook after landing when flight is no longer 'live')
- Extend KV TTL from 7d to 30d to protect the 500 req/month free quota
- Worker acts as HTTPHTTPS proxy: App always uses HTTPS to reach Worker,
  Worker calls AviationStack via plain http:// (free-tier limitation),
  bypassing iOS ATS and Android Cleartext Traffic restrictions
- Add X-Cache: HIT/MISS response header for cache verification
- Validate date param format (YYYY-MM-DD) at Worker level
- Rename env secret AVIATIONSTACK_KEY  AVIATIONSTACK_API_KEY
- No App-side changes needed: ApiService.ts date format already YYYY-MM-DD

* fix(worker): replace broken ICAO slice with native flight_icao param + add limit=1

Bug: CCA1501  slice(0,2)+slice(3)  CC1501 (Macair), causing 100% miss
     for all Chinese 3-letter ICAO designators (CCA/CES/CSN etc.)

Fix: detect IATA(2-letter) vs ICAO(3-letter) via regex and dynamically
     choose flight_iata vs flight_icao AviationStack param  no string
     manipulation, AviationStack handles the lookup natively.

Also: add &limit=1 to minimise JSON payload size and response latency.

* fix(worker): use https:// for AviationStack per official docs (Basic Plan+)

Official API docs clearly state base URL is https://api.aviationstack.com/v1/
The previous http:// assumption was based on outdated free-tier reports.
Basic Plan and above supports HTTPS natively  full end-to-end TLS now.

* security: remove plaintext API key from wrangler.toml

Key must be stored as an encrypted Cloudflare Secret, not a [vars] entry.
Use: wrangler secret put AVIATIONSTACK_API_KEY

* chore: update compatibility_date to 2026-03-05; ignore .wrangler/ dir

* chore: ignore wrangler local cache dir

* feat(worker): enhance flight data gateway with multi-tier fallback and API key management

* Release/pre launch deployment (#12)

* Feature/aviationstack primary (#13)

* feat(worker): switch primary API to AviationStack with flight_date + 30d KV cache

- Remove AirLabs dependency; AviationStack is now the sole upstream API
- Add flight_date param to enable historical flight queries (key use case:
  pilots fill logbook after landing when flight is no longer 'live')
- Extend KV TTL from 7d to 30d to protect the 500 req/month free quota
- Worker acts as HTTPHTTPS proxy: App always uses HTTPS to reach Worker,
  Worker calls AviationStack via plain http:// (free-tier limitation),
  bypassing iOS ATS and Android Cleartext Traffic restrictions
- Add X-Cache: HIT/MISS response header for cache verification
- Validate date param format (YYYY-MM-DD) at Worker level
- Rename env secret AVIATIONSTACK_KEY  AVIATIONSTACK_API_KEY
- No App-side changes needed: ApiService.ts date format already YYYY-MM-DD

* fix(worker): replace broken ICAO slice with native flight_icao param + add limit=1

Bug: CCA1501  slice(0,2)+slice(3)  CC1501 (Macair), causing 100% miss
     for all Chinese 3-letter ICAO designators (CCA/CES/CSN etc.)

Fix: detect IATA(2-letter) vs ICAO(3-letter) via regex and dynamically
     choose flight_iata vs flight_icao AviationStack param  no string
     manipulation, AviationStack handles the lookup natively.

Also: add &limit=1 to minimise JSON payload size and response latency.

* fix(worker): use https:// for AviationStack per official docs (Basic Plan+)

Official API docs clearly state base URL is https://api.aviationstack.com/v1/
The previous http:// assumption was based on outdated free-tier reports.
Basic Plan and above supports HTTPS natively  full end-to-end TLS now.

* security: remove plaintext API key from wrangler.toml

Key must be stored as an encrypted Cloudflare Secret, not a [vars] entry.
Use: wrangler secret put AVIATIONSTACK_API_KEY

* chore: ignore wrangler local cache dir

commit.message:
fix(pb): clear actor reschedule ts on alloc (#4338)

* chore: deploy patch

* feat: add KV range operations (listRange, deleteRange, scan) (#4372)

* fix: correct anyhow imports and document KV range operation limits

* fix(kv): enforce default list limit of 16384 in file-system and cloudflare drivers

* chore(kv): remove scan alias for listRange

* fix(kv): bump TS runner protocol version to 7 and restore published bare schemas

The TypeScript runner's PROTOCOL_VERSION was still 6 while the protocol
file had been regenerated with v7 wire format, causing a wire mismatch.
Also restores v4/v5/v6.bare to their published state and adds v7.bare as
the new schema version. Updates CLAUDE.md with a rule to keep both Rust
and TypeScript protocol versions in sync.

* Update landing page title and subtitle (#4377)

* chore(website): update landing page title and subtitle

* chore(website): expand subtitle with AI agents and use cases

* feat(website): add benchmarks and dynamic actors sections to landing page

* chore: sync README with landing page and update website copy

* refactor(workflow-engine): optimize loop history pruning (#4370)

* fix: simplify loop history management and defer checkpoint flushes

Consolidate historyEvery and historyKeep config options into commitInterval to simplify the API. Add incremental compaction tracking via lastForgottenUpTo to avoid re-scanning already-forgotten iterations. Defer checkpoint flushes to run in parallel with the next iteration's user code. Introduce PendingDeletions interface for collecting deletions to execute alongside checkpoint writes. Expand test coverage for compaction behavior, crash recovery, and queue timeout edge cases. Update all documentation to reflect the simplified API.

* refactor(workflow-engine): rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval and add historySize

Rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval to clarify that it controls how often old iterations are pruned. Add separate historySize option to control how many iterations are retained, defaulting to historyPruneInterval. Rename internal method collectLoopCompaction to collectLoopPruning and update all terminology from compact/checkpoint to prune throughout comments, tests, and docs.

* refactor(workflow-engine): use range deletions for loop history pruning

Replace per-iteration prefix deletes with a single deleteRange call
and track lastPrunedUpTo to avoid redundant re-scans. Add deleteRange
to EngineDriver interface with implementations in both test and
production drivers.

* refactor: explicit protocol version conversions (#4380)

* refactor: replace serialize-deserialize pattern with explicit field-by-field version conversions

Replace the fragile convert_wire_compatible helper (which serialized then deserialized between types) with explicit, transparent field-by-field conversions for all version transitions. Collapse wire-compatible version ranges into single enum variants to reduce unnecessary indirection. Add engine/CLAUDE.md to document the VBARE schema migration pattern and the anti-pattern to avoid.

Replace chat room and simplified agent examples with a realistic AI agent demonstrating the run handler with queue-driven message processing, streaming responses, and real-time event broadcasting. This better showcases Rivet's long-running stateful process model compared to simple action-based patterns.

Updates both the README hero example and the ProblemSection landing page use case.

repository.name: rivet
repository.description: Rivet Actors are the primitive for stateful workloads. Built for AI agents, collaborative apps, and durable execution.

commit.message:
fix(api): properly dedup name list responses (#4337)

* chore: deploy patch

* feat: add KV range operations (listRange, deleteRange, scan) (#4372)

* fix: correct anyhow imports and document KV range operation limits

* fix(kv): enforce default list limit of 16384 in file-system and cloudflare drivers

* chore(kv): remove scan alias for listRange

* fix(kv): bump TS runner protocol version to 7 and restore published bare schemas

The TypeScript runner's PROTOCOL_VERSION was still 6 while the protocol
file had been regenerated with v7 wire format, causing a wire mismatch.
Also restores v4/v5/v6.bare to their published state and adds v7.bare as
the new schema version. Updates CLAUDE.md with a rule to keep both Rust
and TypeScript protocol versions in sync.

* Update landing page title and subtitle (#4377)

* chore(website): update landing page title and subtitle

* chore(website): expand subtitle with AI agents and use cases

* feat(website): add benchmarks and dynamic actors sections to landing page

* chore: sync README with landing page and update website copy

* refactor(workflow-engine): optimize loop history pruning (#4370)

* fix: simplify loop history management and defer checkpoint flushes

Consolidate historyEvery and historyKeep config options into commitInterval to simplify the API. Add incremental compaction tracking via lastForgottenUpTo to avoid re-scanning already-forgotten iterations. Defer checkpoint flushes to run in parallel with the next iteration's user code. Introduce PendingDeletions interface for collecting deletions to execute alongside checkpoint writes. Expand test coverage for compaction behavior, crash recovery, and queue timeout edge cases. Update all documentation to reflect the simplified API.

* refactor(workflow-engine): rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval and add historySize

Rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval to clarify that it controls how often old iterations are pruned. Add separate historySize option to control how many iterations are retained, defaulting to historyPruneInterval. Rename internal method collectLoopCompaction to collectLoopPruning and update all terminology from compact/checkpoint to prune throughout comments, tests, and docs.

* refactor(workflow-engine): use range deletions for loop history pruning

Replace per-iteration prefix deletes with a single deleteRange call
and track lastPrunedUpTo to avoid redundant re-scans. Add deleteRange
to EngineDriver interface with implementations in both test and
production drivers.

* refactor: explicit protocol version conversions (#4380)

* refactor: replace serialize-deserialize pattern with explicit field-by-field version conversions

Replace the fragile convert_wire_compatible helper (which serialized then deserialized between types) with explicit, transparent field-by-field conversions for all version transitions. Collapse wire-compatible version ranges into single enum variants to reduce unnecessary indirection. Add engine/CLAUDE.md to document the VBARE schema migration pattern and the anti-pattern to avoid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update stale comment referencing v6 in v7_to_v4 function

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update AI agent examples to showcase run handler pattern (#4382)

Replace chat room and simplified agent examples with a realistic AI agent demonstrating the run handler with queue-driven message processing, streaming responses, and real-time event broadcasting. This better showcases Rivet's long-running stateful process model compared to simple action-based patterns.

Updates both the README hero example and the ProblemSection landing page use case.

Co-authored-by: jog1t <39823706+jog1t@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: flatten runtime config

* feat: add KV range operations (listRange, deleteRange, scan) (#4372)

* fix: correct anyhow imports and document KV range operation limits

* fix(kv): enforce default list limit of 16384 in file-system and cloudflare drivers

* chore(kv): remove scan alias for listRange

* fix(kv): bump TS runner protocol version to 7 and restore published bare schemas

The TypeScript runner's PROTOCOL_VERSION was still 6 while the protocol
file had been regenerated with v7 wire format, causing a wire mismatch.
Also restores v4/v5/v6.bare to their published state and adds v7.bare as
the new schema version. Updates CLAUDE.md with a rule to keep both Rust
and TypeScript protocol versions in sync.

* refactor(workflow-engine): optimize loop history pruning (#4370)

* fix: simplify loop history management and defer checkpoint flushes

Consolidate historyEvery and historyKeep config options into commitInterval to simplify the API. Add incremental compaction tracking via lastForgottenUpTo to avoid re-scanning already-forgotten iterations. Defer checkpoint flushes to run in parallel with the next iteration's user code. Introduce PendingDeletions interface for collecting deletions to execute alongside checkpoint writes. Expand test coverage for compaction behavior, crash recovery, and queue timeout edge cases. Update all documentation to reflect the simplified API.

* refactor(workflow-engine): rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval and add historySize

Rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval to clarify that it controls how often old iterations are pruned. Add separate historySize option to control how many iterations are retained, defaulting to historyPruneInterval. Rename internal method collectLoopCompaction to collectLoopPruning and update all terminology from compact/checkpoint to prune throughout comments, tests, and docs.

* refactor(workflow-engine): use range deletions for loop history pruning

Replace per-iteration prefix deletes with a single deleteRange call
and track lastPrunedUpTo to avoid redundant re-scans. Add deleteRange
to EngineDriver interface with implementations in both test and
production drivers.

* fix(ci): stabilize pr actions (#4381)

* fix(ci): stabilize pr actions

* fix(swift): mark test suite sendable

* fix(swift): avoid isolated deinit

* chore(ci): disable swift tests

* chore(ci): restore full frontend checks

* fix(ci): restore full repo checks

* fix(workflow-engine): align biome format

* feat(frontend): polish onboarding UI (#4376)

* feat(frontend): make onboarding local-first

Add two new onboarding steps before cloud deployment:
1. "Set up your project" - clone example and run locally (manual) or
   MCP/skills setup with agent instructions (agent flow)
2. "Explore Rivet" - RivetKit Inspector info and links to docs/examples

Move SkillsSetup and TemplateSetup from BackendSetup to the new local
step. BackendSetup now focuses solely on cloud deployment configuration.

Also fix www.rivet.dev -> rivet.dev and rivet-gg -> rivet-dev URLs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(rivetkit): add "start key" and "end key" to validateKvKey label type

The KV range operations added in #4372 pass "start key" and "end key"
as keyLabel arguments but the type only accepted "key" | "prefix key".

repository.name: rivet
repository.description: Rivet Actors are the primitive for stateful workloads. Built for AI agents, collaborative apps, and durable execution.

commit.message:
fix(ups): formalize subjects (#4334)

* chore: deploy patch

* feat: add KV range operations (listRange, deleteRange, scan) (#4372)

* fix: correct anyhow imports and document KV range operation limits

* fix(kv): enforce default list limit of 16384 in file-system and cloudflare drivers

* chore(kv): remove scan alias for listRange

* fix(kv): bump TS runner protocol version to 7 and restore published bare schemas

The TypeScript runner's PROTOCOL_VERSION was still 6 while the protocol
file had been regenerated with v7 wire format, causing a wire mismatch.
Also restores v4/v5/v6.bare to their published state and adds v7.bare as
the new schema version. Updates CLAUDE.md with a rule to keep both Rust
and TypeScript protocol versions in sync.

* Update landing page title and subtitle (#4377)

* chore(website): update landing page title and subtitle

* chore(website): expand subtitle with AI agents and use cases

* feat(website): add benchmarks and dynamic actors sections to landing page

* chore: sync README with landing page and update website copy

* refactor(workflow-engine): optimize loop history pruning (#4370)

* fix: simplify loop history management and defer checkpoint flushes

Consolidate historyEvery and historyKeep config options into commitInterval to simplify the API. Add incremental compaction tracking via lastForgottenUpTo to avoid re-scanning already-forgotten iterations. Defer checkpoint flushes to run in parallel with the next iteration's user code. Introduce PendingDeletions interface for collecting deletions to execute alongside checkpoint writes. Expand test coverage for compaction behavior, crash recovery, and queue timeout edge cases. Update all documentation to reflect the simplified API.

* refactor(workflow-engine): rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval and add historySize

Rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval to clarify that it controls how often old iterations are pruned. Add separate historySize option to control how many iterations are retained, defaulting to historyPruneInterval. Rename internal method collectLoopCompaction to collectLoopPruning and update all terminology from compact/checkpoint to prune throughout comments, tests, and docs.

* refactor(workflow-engine): use range deletions for loop history pruning

Replace per-iteration prefix deletes with a single deleteRange call
and track lastPrunedUpTo to avoid redundant re-scans. Add deleteRange
to EngineDriver interface with implementations in both test and
production drivers.

* refactor: explicit protocol version conversions (#4380)

* refactor: replace serialize-deserialize pattern with explicit field-by-field version conversions

Replace the fragile convert_wire_compatible helper (which serialized then deserialized between types) with explicit, transparent field-by-field conversions for all version transitions. Collapse wire-compatible version ranges into single enum variants to reduce unnecessary indirection. Add engine/CLAUDE.md to document the VBARE schema migration pattern and the anti-pattern to avoid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update stale comment referencing v6 in v7_to_v4 function

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update AI agent examples to showcase run handler pattern (#4382)

Replace chat room and simplified agent examples with a realistic AI agent demonstrating the run handler with queue-driven message processing, streaming responses, and real-time event broadcasting. This better showcases Rivet's long-running stateful process model compared to simple action-based patterns.

Updates both the README hero example and the ProblemSection landing page use case.

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): stabilize pr actions (#4381)

* fix(ci): stabilize pr actions

* fix(swift): mark test suite sendable

* fix(swift): avoid isolated deinit

* chore(ci): disable swift tests

* chore(ci): restore full frontend checks

* chore: flatten runtime config

* feat: add KV range operations (listRange, deleteRange, scan) (#4372)

* fix: correct anyhow imports and document KV range operation limits

* fix(kv): enforce default list limit of 16384 in file-system and cloudflare drivers

* chore(kv): remove scan alias for listRange

* fix(kv): bump TS runner protocol version to 7 and restore published bare schemas

The TypeScript runner's PROTOCOL_VERSION was still 6 while the protocol
file had been regenerated with v7 wire format, causing a wire mismatch.
Also restores v4/v5/v6.bare to their published state and adds v7.bare as
the new schema version. Updates CLAUDE.md with a rule to keep both Rust
and TypeScript protocol versions in sync.

* refactor(workflow-engine): optimize loop history pruning (#4370)

* fix: simplify loop history management and defer checkpoint flushes

Consolidate historyEvery and historyKeep config options into commitInterval to simplify the API. Add incremental compaction tracking via lastForgottenUpTo to avoid re-scanning already-forgotten iterations. Defer checkpoint flushes to run in parallel with the next iteration's user code. Introduce PendingDeletions interface for collecting deletions to execute alongside checkpoint writes. Expand test coverage for compaction behavior, crash recovery, and queue timeout edge cases. Update all documentation to reflect the simplified API.

* refactor(workflow-engine): rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval and add historySize

Rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval to clarify that it controls how often old iterations are pruned. Add separate historySize option to control how many iterations are retained, defaulting to historyPruneInterval. Rename internal method collectLoopCompaction to collectLoopPruning and update all terminology from compact/checkpoint to prune throughout comments, tests, and docs.

* refactor(workflow-engine): use range deletions for loop history pruning

Replace per-iteration prefix deletes with a single deleteRange call
and track lastPrunedUpTo to avoid redundant re-scans. Add deleteRange
to EngineDriver interface with implementations in both test and
production drivers.

Co-authored-by: Jordan <devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix fuzz test crash caused by Zig 0.15.2 self-hosted backend bug (#8)

## Summary

Fixes the fuzz test CI failure caused by a known Zig 0.15.2 bug
([ziglang/zig#23423](https://github.com/ziglang/zig/issues/23423)).

## Root Cause

The Zig self-hosted x86 backend (used by default in Debug builds)
doesn't emit the sanitizer-coverage instrumentation sections
(`__sancov_pcs1` / `__sancov_cntrs`) that the build runner's fuzz
infrastructure requires. When the fuzzer tries to process coverage data,
it finds an empty program counter array and crashes with:

```
thread 2934 panic: start index 1 is larger than end index 0
std/Build/Fuzz.zig:429:17: in addEntryPoint
    for (pcs[1..], 1..) |elem_addr, i| {
```

This affects all Zig 0.15.x versions and is also reported as
[#25798](https://github.com/ziglang/zig/issues/25798) and
[#25470](https://github.com/ziglang/zig/issues/25470).

## Fix

1. **`build.zig`**: Added a dedicated `fuzz` build step that:
   - Forces `use_llvm = true` to ensure proper coverage instrumentation
- Uses `ReleaseSafe` optimization (recommended for fuzzing throughput)
   - Filters to only fuzz test functions (those named `fuzz:*`)

2. **`.github/workflows/fuzz.yml`**: Changed from `zig build test
--fuzz` to `zig build fuzz --fuzz`

## Verification

- Reproduced the crash locally with the original configuration
- Verified the fix: fuzz tests run without crashing (exit code 124 from
timeout = success)
- Verified existing unit tests (`zig build test`) still pass

---------

commit.message:
chore: bump rivetkit protocol version (#4329)

* chore: deploy patch

* feat: add KV range operations (listRange, deleteRange, scan) (#4372)

* fix: correct anyhow imports and document KV range operation limits

* fix(kv): enforce default list limit of 16384 in file-system and cloudflare drivers

* chore(kv): remove scan alias for listRange

* fix(kv): bump TS runner protocol version to 7 and restore published bare schemas

The TypeScript runner's PROTOCOL_VERSION was still 6 while the protocol
file had been regenerated with v7 wire format, causing a wire mismatch.
Also restores v4/v5/v6.bare to their published state and adds v7.bare as
the new schema version. Updates CLAUDE.md with a rule to keep both Rust
and TypeScript protocol versions in sync.

* Update landing page title and subtitle (#4377)

* chore(website): update landing page title and subtitle

* chore(website): expand subtitle with AI agents and use cases

* feat(website): add benchmarks and dynamic actors sections to landing page

* chore: sync README with landing page and update website copy

* refactor(workflow-engine): optimize loop history pruning (#4370)

* fix: simplify loop history management and defer checkpoint flushes

Consolidate historyEvery and historyKeep config options into commitInterval to simplify the API. Add incremental compaction tracking via lastForgottenUpTo to avoid re-scanning already-forgotten iterations. Defer checkpoint flushes to run in parallel with the next iteration's user code. Introduce PendingDeletions interface for collecting deletions to execute alongside checkpoint writes. Expand test coverage for compaction behavior, crash recovery, and queue timeout edge cases. Update all documentation to reflect the simplified API.

* refactor(workflow-engine): rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval and add historySize

Rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval to clarify that it controls how often old iterations are pruned. Add separate historySize option to control how many iterations are retained, defaulting to historyPruneInterval. Rename internal method collectLoopCompaction to collectLoopPruning and update all terminology from compact/checkpoint to prune throughout comments, tests, and docs.

* refactor(workflow-engine): use range deletions for loop history pruning

Replace per-iteration prefix deletes with a single deleteRange call
and track lastPrunedUpTo to avoid redundant re-scans. Add deleteRange
to EngineDriver interface with implementations in both test and
production drivers.

* refactor: explicit protocol version conversions (#4380)

* refactor: replace serialize-deserialize pattern with explicit field-by-field version conversions

Replace the fragile convert_wire_compatible helper (which serialized then deserialized between types) with explicit, transparent field-by-field conversions for all version transitions. Collapse wire-compatible version ranges into single enum variants to reduce unnecessary indirection. Add engine/CLAUDE.md to document the VBARE schema migration pattern and the anti-pattern to avoid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update stale comment referencing v6 in v7_to_v4 function

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update AI agent examples to showcase run handler pattern (#4382)

Replace chat room and simplified agent examples with a realistic AI agent demonstrating the run handler with queue-driven message processing, streaming responses, and real-time event broadcasting. Updates both the README hero example and the ProblemSection landing page use case.

* chore: flatten runtime config

* feat: add KV range operations (listRange, deleteRange, scan) (#4372)

* fix: correct anyhow imports and document KV range operation limits

* fix(kv): enforce default list limit of 16384 in file-system and cloudflare drivers

* chore(kv): remove scan alias for listRange

* fix(kv): bump TS runner protocol version to 7 and restore published bare schemas

The TypeScript runner's PROTOCOL_VERSION was still 6 while the protocol
file had been regenerated with v7 wire format, causing a wire mismatch.
Also restores v4/v5/v6.bare to their published state and adds v7.bare as
the new schema version. Updates CLAUDE.md with a rule to keep both Rust
and TypeScript protocol versions in sync.

* refactor(workflow-engine): optimize loop history pruning (#4370)

* fix: simplify loop history management and defer checkpoint flushes

Consolidate historyEvery and historyKeep config options into commitInterval to simplify the API. Add incremental compaction tracking via lastForgottenUpTo to avoid re-scanning already-forgotten iterations. Defer checkpoint flushes to run in parallel with the next iteration's user code. Introduce PendingDeletions interface for collecting deletions to execute alongside checkpoint writes. Expand test coverage for compaction behavior, crash recovery, and queue timeout edge cases. Update all documentation to reflect the simplified API.

* refactor(workflow-engine): rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval and add historySize

Rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval to clarify that it controls how often old iterations are pruned. Add separate historySize option to control how many iterations are retained, defaulting to historyPruneInterval. Rename internal method collectLoopCompaction to collectLoopPruning and update all terminology from compact/checkpoint to prune throughout comments, tests, and docs.

* refactor(workflow-engine): use range deletions for loop history pruning

Replace per-iteration prefix deletes with a single deleteRange call
and track lastPrunedUpTo to avoid redundant re-scans. Add deleteRange
to EngineDriver interface with implementations in both test and
production drivers.

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: tfritzy <23142109+tfritzy@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(udb): add txn tagging (#4328)

* chore: deploy patch

* feat: add KV range operations (listRange, deleteRange, scan) (#4372)

* fix: correct anyhow imports and document KV range operation limits

* fix(kv): enforce default list limit of 16384 in file-system and cloudflare drivers

* chore(kv): remove scan alias for listRange

* fix(kv): bump TS runner protocol version to 7 and restore published bare schemas

The TypeScript runner's PROTOCOL_VERSION was still 6 while the protocol
file had been regenerated with v7 wire format, causing a wire mismatch.
Also restores v4/v5/v6.bare to their published state and adds v7.bare as
the new schema version. Updates CLAUDE.md with a rule to keep both Rust
and TypeScript protocol versions in sync.

* Update landing page title and subtitle (#4377)

* chore(website): update landing page title and subtitle

* chore(website): expand subtitle with AI agents and use cases

* feat(website): add benchmarks and dynamic actors sections to landing page

* chore: sync README with landing page and update website copy

* refactor(workflow-engine): optimize loop history pruning (#4370)

* fix: simplify loop history management and defer checkpoint flushes

Consolidate historyEvery and historyKeep config options into commitInterval to simplify the API. Add incremental compaction tracking via lastForgottenUpTo to avoid re-scanning already-forgotten iterations. Defer checkpoint flushes to run in parallel with the next iteration's user code. Introduce PendingDeletions interface for collecting deletions to execute alongside checkpoint writes. Expand test coverage for compaction behavior, crash recovery, and queue timeout edge cases. Update all documentation to reflect the simplified API.

* refactor(workflow-engine): rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval and add historySize

Rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval to clarify that it controls how often old iterations are pruned. Add separate historySize option to control how many iterations are retained, defaulting to historyPruneInterval. Rename internal method collectLoopCompaction to collectLoopPruning and update all terminology from compact/checkpoint to prune throughout comments, tests, and docs.

* refactor(workflow-engine): use range deletions for loop history pruning

Replace per-iteration prefix deletes with a single deleteRange call
and track lastPrunedUpTo to avoid redundant re-scans. Add deleteRange
to EngineDriver interface with implementations in both test and
production drivers.

* refactor: explicit protocol version conversions (#4380)

* refactor: replace serialize-deserialize pattern with explicit field-by-field version conversions

Replace the fragile convert_wire_compatible helper (which serialized then deserialized between types) with explicit, transparent field-by-field conversions for all version transitions. Collapse wire-compatible version ranges into single enum variants to reduce unnecessary indirection. Add engine/CLAUDE.md to document the VBARE schema migration pattern and the anti-pattern to avoid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update stale comment referencing v6 in v7_to_v4 function

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update AI agent examples to showcase run handler pattern (#4382)

Replace chat room and simplified agent examples with a realistic AI agent demonstrating the run handler with queue-driven message processing, streaming responses, and real-time event broadcasting. This better showcases Rivet's long-running stateful process model compared to simple action-based patterns.

Updates both the README hero example and the ProblemSection landing page use case.

repository.name: rivet
repository.description: Rivet Actors are the primitive for stateful workloads. Built for AI agents, collaborative apps, and durable execution.

commit.message:
fix(api): make actor destroy wait for ack (#4327)

* chore: deploy patch

* feat: add KV range operations (listRange, deleteRange, scan) (#4372)

* fix: correct anyhow imports and document KV range operation limits

* fix(kv): enforce default list limit of 16384 in file-system and cloudflare drivers

* chore(kv): remove scan alias for listRange

* fix(kv): bump TS runner protocol version to 7 and restore published bare schemas

The TypeScript runner's PROTOCOL_VERSION was still 6 while the protocol
file had been regenerated with v7 wire format, causing a wire mismatch.
Also restores v4/v5/v6.bare to their published state and adds v7.bare as
the new schema version. Updates CLAUDE.md with a rule to keep both Rust
and TypeScript protocol versions in sync.

* Update landing page title and subtitle (#4377)

* chore(website): update landing page title and subtitle

* chore(website): expand subtitle with AI agents and use cases

* feat(website): add benchmarks and dynamic actors sections to landing page

* chore: sync README with landing page and update website copy

* refactor(workflow-engine): optimize loop history pruning (#4370)

* fix: simplify loop history management and defer checkpoint flushes

Consolidate historyEvery and historyKeep config options into commitInterval to simplify the API. Add incremental compaction tracking via lastForgottenUpTo to avoid re-scanning already-forgotten iterations. Defer checkpoint flushes to run in parallel with the next iteration's user code. Introduce PendingDeletions interface for collecting deletions to execute alongside checkpoint writes. Expand test coverage for compaction behavior, crash recovery, and queue timeout edge cases. Update all documentation to reflect the simplified API.

* refactor(workflow-engine): rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval and add historySize

Rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval to clarify that it controls how often old iterations are pruned. Add separate historySize option to control how many iterations are retained, defaulting to historyPruneInterval. Rename internal method collectLoopCompaction to collectLoopPruning and update all terminology from compact/checkpoint to prune throughout comments, tests, and docs.

* refactor(workflow-engine): use range deletions for loop history pruning

Replace per-iteration prefix deletes with a single deleteRange call
and track lastPrunedUpTo to avoid redundant re-scans. Add deleteRange
to EngineDriver interface with implementations in both test and
production drivers.

* refactor: explicit protocol version conversions (#4380)

* refactor: replace serialize-deserialize pattern with explicit field-by-field version conversions

Replace the fragile convert_wire_compatible helper (which serialized then deserialized between types) with explicit, transparent field-by-field conversions for all version transitions. Collapse wire-compatible version ranges into single enum variants to reduce unnecessary indirection. Add engine/CLAUDE.md to document the VBARE schema migration pattern and the anti-pattern to avoid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update stale comment referencing v6 in v7_to_v4 function

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update AI agent examples to showcase run handler pattern (#4382)

Replace chat room and simplified agent examples with a realistic AI agent demonstrating the run handler with queue-driven message processing, streaming responses, and real-time event broadcasting. This better showcases Rivet's long-running stateful process model compared to simple action-based patterns.

Updates both the README hero example and the ProblemSection landing page use case.

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

2. "Explore Rivet" - RivetKit Inspector info and links to docs/examples

Move SkillsSetup and TemplateSetup from BackendSetup to the new local

Also fix www.rivet.dev -> rivet.dev and rivet-gg -> rivet-dev URLs.

* chore: flatten runtime config

* feat: add KV range operations (listRange, deleteRange, scan) (#4372)

* fix: correct anyhow imports and document KV range operation limits

* fix(kv): enforce default list limit of 16384 in file-system and cloudflare drivers

* chore(kv): remove scan alias for listRange

* fix(kv): bump TS runner protocol version to 7 and restore published bare schemas

The TypeScript runner's PROTOCOL_VERSION was still 6 while the protocol
file had been regenerated with v7 wire format, causing a wire mismatch.
Also restores v4/v5/v6.bare to their published state and adds v7.bare as
the new schema version. Updates CLAUDE.md with a rule to keep both Rust
and TypeScript protocol versions in sync.

* refactor(workflow-engine): optimize loop history pruning (#4370)

* fix: simplify loop history management and defer checkpoint flushes

Consolidate historyEvery and historyKeep config options into commitInterval to simplify the API. Add incremental compaction tracking via lastForgottenUpTo to avoid re-scanning already-forgotten iterations. Defer checkpoint flushes to run in parallel with the next iteration's user code. Introduce PendingDeletions interface for collecting deletions to execute alongside checkpoint writes. Expand test coverage for compaction behavior, crash recovery, and queue timeout edge cases. Update all documentation to reflect the simplified API.

* refactor(workflow-engine): rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval and add historySize

Rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval to clarify that it controls how often old iterations are pruned. Add separate historySize option to control how many iterations are retained, defaulting to historyPruneInterval. Rename internal method collectLoopCompaction to collectLoopPruning and update all terminology from compact/checkpoint to prune throughout comments, tests, and docs.

* refactor(workflow-engine): use range deletions for loop history pruning

Replace per-iteration prefix deletes with a single deleteRange call
and track lastPrunedUpTo to avoid redundant re-scans. Add deleteRange
to EngineDriver interface with implementations in both test and
production drivers.

* fix(ci): stabilize pr actions (#4381)

* fix(ci): stabilize pr actions

* fix(swift): mark test suite sendable

* fix(swift): avoid isolated deinit

* chore(ci): disable swift tests

* chore(ci): restore full frontend checks

* fix(ci): restore full repo checks

* fix(workflow-engine): align biome format

* feat(frontend): polish onboarding UI (#4376)

* feat(frontend): make onboarding local-first

Add two new onboarding steps before cloud deployment:
1. "Set up your project" - clone example and run locally (manual) or
   MCP/skills setup with agent instructions (agent flow)
2. "Explore Rivet" - RivetKit Inspector info and links to docs/examples

Move SkillsSetup and TemplateSetup from BackendSetup to the new local
step. BackendSetup now focuses solely on cloud deployment configuration.

Also fix www.rivet.dev -> rivet.dev and rivet-gg -> rivet-dev URLs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(rivetkit): add "start key" and "end key" to validateKvKey label type

The KV range operations added in #4372 pass "start key" and "end key"
as keyLabel arguments but the type only accepted "key" | "prefix key".

repository.name: rivet
repository.description: Rivet Actors are the primitive for stateful workloads. Built for AI agents, collaborative apps, and durable execution.

commit.message:
chore: flatten runtime config (#4326)

* refactor(workflow-engine): optimize loop history pruning (#4370)

* fix: simplify loop history management and defer checkpoint flushes

Consolidate historyEvery and historyKeep config options into commitInterval to simplify the API. Add incremental compaction tracking via lastForgottenUpTo to avoid re-scanning already-forgotten iterations. Defer checkpoint flushes to run in parallel with the next iteration's user code. Introduce PendingDeletions interface for collecting deletions to execute alongside checkpoint writes. Expand test coverage for compaction behavior, crash recovery, and queue timeout edge cases. Update all documentation to reflect the simplified API.

* refactor(workflow-engine): rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval and add historySize

Rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval to clarify that it controls how often old iterations are pruned. Add separate historySize option to control how many iterations are retained, defaulting to historyPruneInterval. Rename internal method collectLoopCompaction to collectLoopPruning and update all terminology from compact/checkpoint to prune throughout comments, tests, and docs.

* refactor(workflow-engine): use range deletions for loop history pruning

Replace per-iteration prefix deletes with a single deleteRange call
and track lastPrunedUpTo to avoid redundant re-scans. Add deleteRange
to EngineDriver interface with implementations in both test and
production drivers.

* fix(ci): stabilize pr actions (#4381)

* fix(ci): stabilize pr actions

* fix(swift): mark test suite sendable

* fix(swift): avoid isolated deinit

* chore(ci): disable swift tests

* chore(ci): restore full frontend checks

* fix(ci): restore full repo checks

* fix(workflow-engine): align biome format

* feat(frontend): polish onboarding UI (#4376)

* feat(frontend): make onboarding local-first

Add two new onboarding steps before cloud deployment:
1. "Set up your project" - clone example and run locally (manual) or
   MCP/skills setup with agent instructions (agent flow)
2. "Explore Rivet" - RivetKit Inspector info and links to docs/examples

Move SkillsSetup and TemplateSetup from BackendSetup to the new local
step. BackendSetup now focuses solely on cloud deployment configuration.

Also fix www.rivet.dev -> rivet.dev and rivet-gg -> rivet-dev URLs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(rivetkit): add "start key" and "end key" to validateKvKey label type

The KV range operations added in #4372 pass "start key" and "end key"
as keyLabel arguments but the type only accepted "key" | "prefix key".

* chore: flatten runtime config

* feat: add KV range operations (listRange, deleteRange, scan) (#4372)

* fix: correct anyhow imports and document KV range operation limits

* fix(kv): enforce default list limit of 16384 in file-system and cloudflare drivers

* chore(kv): remove scan alias for listRange

* fix(kv): bump TS runner protocol version to 7 and restore published bare schemas

The TypeScript runner's PROTOCOL_VERSION was still 6 while the protocol
file had been regenerated with v7 wire format, causing a wire mismatch.
Also restores v4/v5/v6.bare to their published state and adds v7.bare as
the new schema version. Updates CLAUDE.md with a rule to keep both Rust
and TypeScript protocol versions in sync.

* refactor(workflow-engine): optimize loop history pruning (#4370)

* fix: simplify loop history management and defer checkpoint flushes

Consolidate historyEvery and historyKeep config options into commitInterval to simplify the API. Add incremental compaction tracking via lastForgottenUpTo to avoid re-scanning already-forgotten iterations. Defer checkpoint flushes to run in parallel with the next iteration's user code. Introduce PendingDeletions interface for collecting deletions to execute alongside checkpoint writes. Expand test coverage for compaction behavior, crash recovery, and queue timeout edge cases. Update all documentation to reflect the simplified API.

* refactor(workflow-engine): rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval and add historySize

Rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval to clarify that it controls how often old iterations are pruned. Add separate historySize option to control how many iterations are retained, defaulting to historyPruneInterval. Rename internal method collectLoopCompaction to collectLoopPruning and update all terminology from compact/checkpoint to prune throughout comments, tests, and docs.

* refactor(workflow-engine): use range deletions for loop history pruning

Replace per-iteration prefix deletes with a single deleteRange call
and track lastPrunedUpTo to avoid redundant re-scans. Add deleteRange
to EngineDriver interface with implementations in both test and
production drivers.

Move SkillsSetup and TemplateSetup from BackendSetup to the new local

commit.message:
chore: deploy patch (#4324)

* chore: deploy patch

* feat: add KV range operations (listRange, deleteRange, scan) (#4372)

* fix: correct anyhow imports and document KV range operation limits

* fix(kv): enforce default list limit of 16384 in file-system and cloudflare drivers

* chore(kv): remove scan alias for listRange

* fix(kv): bump TS runner protocol version to 7 and restore published bare schemas

The TypeScript runner's PROTOCOL_VERSION was still 6 while the protocol
file had been regenerated with v7 wire format, causing a wire mismatch.
Also restores v4/v5/v6.bare to their published state and adds v7.bare as
the new schema version. Updates CLAUDE.md with a rule to keep both Rust
and TypeScript protocol versions in sync.

* Update landing page title and subtitle (#4377)

* chore(website): update landing page title and subtitle

* chore(website): expand subtitle with AI agents and use cases

* feat(website): add benchmarks and dynamic actors sections to landing page

* chore: sync README with landing page and update website copy

* refactor(workflow-engine): optimize loop history pruning (#4370)

* fix: simplify loop history management and defer checkpoint flushes

Consolidate historyEvery and historyKeep config options into commitInterval to simplify the API. Add incremental compaction tracking via lastForgottenUpTo to avoid re-scanning already-forgotten iterations. Defer checkpoint flushes to run in parallel with the next iteration's user code. Introduce PendingDeletions interface for collecting deletions to execute alongside checkpoint writes. Expand test coverage for compaction behavior, crash recovery, and queue timeout edge cases. Update all documentation to reflect the simplified API.

* refactor(workflow-engine): rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval and add historySize

Rename checkpointInterval to historyPruneInterval to clarify that it controls how often old iterations are pruned. Add separate historySize option to control how many iterations are retained, defaulting to historyPruneInterval. Rename internal method collectLoopCompaction to collectLoopPruning and update all terminology from compact/checkpoint to prune throughout comments, tests, and docs.

* refactor(workflow-engine): use range deletions for loop history pruning

Replace per-iteration prefix deletes with a single deleteRange call
and track lastPrunedUpTo to avoid redundant re-scans. Add deleteRange
to EngineDriver interface with implementations in both test and
production drivers.

* refactor: explicit protocol version conversions (#4380)

* refactor: replace serialize-deserialize pattern with explicit field-by-field version conversions

Replace the fragile convert_wire_compatible helper (which serialized then deserialized between types) with explicit, transparent field-by-field conversions for all version transitions. Collapse wire-compatible version ranges into single enum variants to reduce unnecessary indirection. Add engine/CLAUDE.md to document the VBARE schema migration pattern and the anti-pattern to avoid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update stale comment referencing v6 in v7_to_v4 function

* docs: update AI agent examples to showcase run handler pattern (#4382)

Replace chat room and simplified agent examples with a realistic AI agent demonstrating the run handler with queue-driven message processing, streaming responses, and real-time event broadcasting. This better showcases Rivet's long-running stateful process model compared to simple action-based patterns.

Updates both the README hero example and the ProblemSection landing page use case.

commit.message:
test: add fuzz tests for metrics and make fuzz target (#115)

Add fuzz tests for metrics package:
- FuzzCounterAdd: counter increment with arbitrary int64 values
- FuzzCounterWithLabels: labeled counter with arbitrary label keys/values
- FuzzGaugeAdd: gauge operations with arbitrary float64 (NaN, Inf, etc)
- FuzzHistogramObserve: histogram observation with arbitrary float64

commit.message:
feat: implement sonde-gateway Phase 2A foundation (#26)

* feat: implement sonde-gateway Phase 2A foundation

Scaffold the sonde-gateway crate and build all 6 foundation modules
with 17 validation tests passing.

Modules:
- crypto.rs: RustCryptoHmac (constant-time verify) + RustCryptoSha256
  implementing sonde-protocol traits
- transport.rs: async Transport trait + MockTransport (inbound queue,
  outbound capture, PeerAddress)
- storage.rs: async Storage trait + InMemoryStorage (tokio RwLock)
- registry.rs: NodeRecord with key_hint lookup, battery/ABI tracking,
  program hash confirmation
- session.rs: Session/SessionManager/SessionState with create/replace,
  sequence verification, state transitions, expiry reaping
- program.rs: ProgramRecord/ProgramLibrary with CBOR image storage,
  SHA-256 hash computation, size enforcement, chunk serving

Tests (17 passing):
- T-0100: no unsolicited transmission
- T-0700/0702/0703: node registry, battery tracking, ABI version
- T-0604/0605/0606/0607/T-1004: session replay protection, replacement,
  wrong seq, no session, timeout
- T-0400/0401/0405/0406/0407: program ingestion, rejection, hash
  identity, hash covers maps, size enforcement
- Crypto: HMAC round-trip, wrong-key rejection, SHA-256 determinism

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR #26 review -- 4 issues

- session.rs: verify_and_advance_seq checks session expiry inline
  (removes expired session and returns SessionError::Expired) instead
  of relying on background reap_expired() call
- session.rs: add SessionError::Expired variant
- program.rs: reject empty images with ProgramError::InvalidImage
- Cargo.toml: remove unused rand dependency
- T-1004 test: verify timeout via verify_and_advance_seq (not manual
  reap), matching the validation spec procedure
- T-0401 test: verify empty image is rejected

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: clarify Transport::recv doc for real vs mock behavior

Real transports await until a frame arrives; test mocks may return
TransportError::NoMoreFrames immediately when the queue is empty.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Automate PyPI publish on push to master

Replaces manual release-triggered workflow with auto-detect:
- On push to master, checks if sun_hit_detector/ or pyproject.toml changed
- If yes: bumps patch version, commits [skip ci], tags, creates release, publishes to PyPI
- If only custom_components/ or other files changed: skips (no unnecessary publish)
- GITHUB_TOKEN pushes don't re-trigger workflows (no infinite loops)

HACS sees new versions via GitHub releases, PyPI gets the core library.

commit.message:
Fix all button failures: consolidate 6 sheet modifiers into one

DashboardView had 6 separate .sheet(isPresented:) modifiers on the same
view — SwiftUI only honors the last one and silently breaks all touch
events on the others. Replace all 6 with a single .sheet(item:) driven
by a DashboardSheet enum. Store booleans (showEndInteractionSheet,
showStartShift) are synced via .onChange. This is the root cause of
every 'buttons not working' report.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Virtual input system review (#2936)

* fix: virtual input system review — 6 fixes for keyboard/mouse overlay gaps

Fixes #2930: Auto-hide virtual keyboard when USB/Bluetooth keyboard connects
- Observe GCKeyboardDidConnect/GCKeyboardDidDisconnect notifications
- Track whether keyboard was auto-hidden so it restores on disconnect

Fixes #2931: Apply effectiveKeyboardOverlayConfig variant/opacity
- Map VirtualKeyboardVariant -> VirtualKeyboardLayout via toLayout()
- Use skin config or user preference for initial layout
- Apply configured opacity to hosting view

Fixes #2932: ensureProperZOrder() now includes virtual input overlays
- Call bringVirtualInputOverlaysToFront() during rotation/layout changes

Fixes #2933: Add virtual mouse show/hide toggle to pause menu
- New coreSupportsVirtualMouse, isVirtualMouseVisible properties
- New showVirtualMouse/hideVirtualMouse/toggleVirtualMouse methods
- VIRTUAL MOUSE button in RetroMenuView CORE tab

Fixes #2934: preferredKeyboardVariant setting now used
- Read on keyboard show, write when user switches layout
- Resolution: skin config > user preference > system default > .full

Fixes #2935: Re-enable onscreenJoypadWithKeyboard logic
- Uncomment adjustJoystick() check for keyboard + setting

Co-authored-by: Joseph Mattiello <jmattiello+wayfair@wayfair.com>

* fix: address Copilot review — keyboard layout priority and joypad visibility

- resolvedKeyboardLayout() now checks: (1) explicit skin JSON config,
  (2) user's preferredKeyboardVariant, (3) DeltaSkinDefaults, (4) .full.
  Previously the user preference was unreachable for systems with a
  default config (DOS, C64, MSX, etc.).

- adjustJoystick() now sets joyPad.isHidden = false when the
  keyboard-connected condition is not met, so the joypad restores
  after a hardware keyboard is disconnected.

Co-authored-by: Joseph Mattiello <jmattiello+wayfair@wayfair.com>

* Update PVUI/Sources/PVUIBase/SwiftUI/DeltaSkins/Models/DeltaSkinTypes.swift

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: JoeMatt <399864+JoeMatt@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address remaining Copilot review comments

- Hide/show joyPad2 alongside joyPad in adjustJoystick() so both
  joystick overlays respect onscreenJoypadWithKeyboard consistently.
- Add @MainActor to the virtual mouse extension so UIKit view
  hierarchy mutations are guaranteed to run on the main thread.
- Change hwKeyboardObservers type from [Any]? to [NSObjectProtocol]?
  for type-safety matching NotificationCenter.addObserver return type.

Co-authored-by: Joseph Mattiello <jmattiello+wayfair@wayfair.com>

* fix: prevent virtual keyboard flash when hardware keyboard is connected

Start hardware keyboard observation before showing the virtual keyboard
overlay in setupVirtualInputOverlaysIfNeeded(). If a hardware keyboard
is already connected (GCKeyboard.coalesced != nil), skip the auto-show
entirely to avoid a visible show-then-hide flicker on launch.

Co-authored-by: Joseph Mattiello <jmattiello+wayfair@wayfair.com>

* fix: prevent virtual keyboard flash when hardware keyboard already connected

Check GCKeyboard.coalesced before showing the virtual keyboard on
launch. Previously the overlay was shown then immediately hidden
when startObservingHardwareKeyboard() detected the existing
hardware keyboard, causing a visible flicker. Now the hardware
keyboard check runs first and skips the show entirely, while still
setting keyboardHiddenByHardware so disconnecting later will
auto-restore the overlay.

Co-authored-by: Joseph Mattiello <jmattiello+wayfair@wayfair.com>

feat: Foundry agents lifecycle - CI/CD best practices demo

Complete educational demo for CI/CD of Microsoft Foundry AI agents.

Infrastructure (Bicep IaC):
- Foundry resource (AIServices + allowProjectManagement)
- Foundry project as child resource
- Model deployments (gpt-4o-mini)
- Key Vault for secrets management
- Subscription-scope orchestrator with dual-pipeline isolation

CI/CD Pipelines:
- GitHub Actions (OIDC auth, deploy agent, run evals)
- Azure DevOps (service connections, same workflow)
- Both pipelines target separate resource groups for isolation

Agent Lifecycle Scripts:
- deploy_agent.py: SDK-driven agent creation from config
- test_agent.py: Interactive agent testing with conversation support
- run_evaluation.py: Automated evaluation with scoring

Documentation:
- MkDocs site with architecture, mental model, quickstart
- Per-pipeline guides (GitHub Actions + Azure DevOps)
- Config schema reference (SCHEMA.md)
- FAQ and troubleshooting

Key insight: Foundry agents have no export/import — CI/CD is
SDK-driven (delete + recreate from code). This demo teaches that.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: add benchmarks for ratelimit, request, and apperror (#110)

Add benchmark tests for ratelimit package:
- BenchmarkAllow_UniqueKeys: many distinct keys (map growth)
- BenchmarkAllowN: multi-token consumption
- BenchmarkLen: state inspection under load

Add benchmark tests for request package:
- BenchmarkParsePagination_Defaults: default pagination path
- BenchmarkOptionalQueryParam/Int: optional param extraction
- BenchmarkParseCommaSeparated/Ints: slice-allocating parsers
- BenchmarkParseSortOrder: field validation with allowed list
- BenchmarkRequireQueryParamInt: int64 parsing from query

Add benchmark tests for apperror package:
- BenchmarkNew/Wrap: error construction
- BenchmarkError_Error/ErrorWrapped: string formatting
- BenchmarkHTTPStatus/PlainError: status code extraction
- BenchmarkIsCode/Wrapped: error code matching

Co-authored-by: Jordan <devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Promote dev → uat (v0.1.4) (#123)

* chore: fix all 56 lint errors across codebase (#118)

* chore: fix all 56 lint errors across codebase

- Remove unused imports: START_GAME, getTilesWhere, TileState,
  PlaceBuildingPayload, ChatPayload, CREATURE_AI, ArraySchema,
  CreatureState
- Remove unused variables: form, playerName (2x), player (2x)
- Remove dead code: unused getSessionId function in e2e tests
- Replace 41 no-explicit-any violations in reconnection.test.ts
  with typed helper functions (asClient, roomInternals) and
  proper RoomTestInternals interface

No game logic or lint config changes. 738 tests passing.

Closes #117

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: update Pemulis history with lint cleanup learnings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <github-copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: include CPU players in lobby room player count

The lobby was explicitly subtracting CPU players from the reported
player count, showing e.g. '1/8' when there was 1 human + 3 CPU.
Now reports total players including CPU opponents.

Closes #121

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Discord notifications on UAT deploy

Multiple pushes to uat during promotion (manual merge + PR merge)
each triggered deploy-uat.yml, causing double Discord notifications.

Add concurrency group with cancel-in-progress so only the latest
push completes the full deploy + notify pipeline.

Closes #119

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: bump version to v0.1.4

---------

* Initial plan

- Replace empty placeholder in AuxButtonGrid with auto-candidates button (✏️)

  from Topology to exclude same row/col/box digits, sets remaining as ACTIVE notes

When disabled (default), the aux grid shows a placeholder.
